response:
Sec. 1  
Allergies to peanuts are immune system reactions to proteins found in peanuts  
These reactions can cause symptoms ranging from mild itching to severe anaphylaxis  
Living underwater is not related to the body's ability to process allergens  
The environment underwater does not eliminate allergens from the air or food  
People with peanut allergies must avoid exposure to peanuts regardless of their location  

Sec. 2  
The question implies a connection between allergies and the ability to live underwater  
This connection does not exist in medical or biological terms  
Allergic reactions are triggered by specific proteins not found in water  
Water does not prevent the body from reacting to allergens in the environment  
Therefore people with peanut allergies can live underwater as long as they avoid peanut exposure
